channel.title: GetDiscoverable.io — Notes on being found
channel.description: Plain-English writing on SEO, AEO, and GEO — how a business gets found by search engines and AI assistants. By Zillah Bahar.

item.title: SEO vs AEO vs GEO: What's the Difference?
item.description: A plain-English guide to the three ways people and AI find you online in 2026 — search engines, answer engines, and generative engines — and why you can no longer optimize for only the first.

item.title: How I Got COLAClear Cited by Name in Google's AI Overview
item.description: A first-person account of the work behind a by-name AI citation — writing for real questions, machine-readable signals (schema.org, llms.txt), AI-crawler access, and earned authority. No budget, no team.

item.title: Why AI Recommends Some Wineries by Name — and Never Mentions Yours
item.description: When a buyer asks ChatGPT for a great bottle, some wineries get named and others vanish. What separates them — machine-readable text, one consistent name, structured data, open crawler access — and how to fix it.
